title: Business survey information | Stats NZ
content:
## Business survey information
Has your business been asked to participate in a Stats NZ survey? Find out why you need to take part, what we do with your information, and how to get help if you need it.
Tēnā koe. Thank you for taking part.

## Why your information is needed
Your information allows Stats NZ to produce statistics about industry and economic trends, production, financial performance, and many other topics.
People in government, business, iwi, community and other organisations need this information. They use it to make decisions that will help our economy and improve the lives of people living in New Zealand.
For example, the Agricultural Production Survey helps government and businesses identify trends in agriculture, horticulture, and forestry.

## Sample of 2023 survey form
Business Operations Survey 2023 is a sample of the 2023 questionnaire.

## The law requires you to take part
The information gathered by Stats NZ business surveys is so important that everyone chosen to take part must do so.
Stats NZ surveys are a request under section 23 of the Data and Statistics Act 2022. Legally anyone who is asked to complete the survey has to answer all mandatory questions before the end of the collection period. Failing to answer the questions is an offence under section 76 of the Act and a conviction could lead to a fine of up to $2000 for an individual and $12,000 for a business or organisation.
If you don't think you can take part in a survey, get in touch to talk about options.
Contact us about a survey
We know how important it is to ensure your information is safe.
Privacy, security, and confidentiality of survey data has information about Stats NZ’s standards for keeping your information private, secure, and confidential.

## Stats can benefit your business
You can use the statistics we produce to help start and grow your business.
Data for business has free information that you can use for:
- assessing future industry trends
- estimating your market share
- assessing consumer spending patterns
- benchmarking your business against other businesses in your industry.
Information is on our website for everyone to use.

## What we do with your information
We collect information and analyse it to produce statistics about New Zealand. These statistics give information about topics like employment, how much households spend, and how satisfied people feel with their lives.
We keep your identity confidential and your data safe. Our published statistics and research do not identify individuals, households, or businesses.
We store data securely. If it’s not needed, we destroy it.
We don't share your information with any other agencies, except if you provide information in one of our two joint surveys.
We have two joint surveys with other agencies.
The Agricultural Production Survey is a joint survey with the Ministry for Primary Industries (MPI). Stats NZ provides the information collected in this survey to MPI.
The Research and Development Survey is a joint survey with the Ministry for Business, Innovation & Employment (MBIE). Stats NZ can provide the information collected in this survey to MBIE.
MPI and MBIE may only use the data to produce statistics, or for research. The statistics or research helps to guide policy and enables government to assess changes in the agricultural sector, and business research and development. MPI and MBIE must ensure individuals and organisations are not identified in published statistics or research. MPI and MBIE may release some information about businesses if authorised by Stats NZ under the Data and Statistics Act 2022.
Contact us about a survey
We sometimes link survey data with other data so decision-makers and researchers can use it.
When we do this we make sure it's safe. We remove details like names and addresses and group the data in a way that stops businesses being identified.

## Why your business was selected
We select business survey participants from a list of businesses in New Zealand. To get an accurate and relevant picture, businesses of all sizes and from all regions are selected.
Your business may have been selected because:
- it represents your industry
- it's a key contributor to an industry
- your industry is small, and all businesses in it need to be surveyed.
Even small businesses may be selected, because information from businesses of all sizes is needed.
Some businesses may be required to participate in more than one survey.

## Making it easier for businesses
Stats NZ surveys as few businesses as possible to produce robust statistics.
Survey questionnaires are as short and simple as possible.
We collect data from other agencies, for example Inland Revenue, to save you time and effort.

## What's involved in taking part
Businesses will be informed they have been selected for a survey. They will receive a letter from us, and/or a questionnaire.
In some cases, a business may get a phone call before receiving the questionnaire.
Business surveys can be completed by filling in paper, editable PDF,  Excel, or online versions of the questionnaire. You’ll find instructions about the mode on the questionnaire you receive.

## You won't be paid for completing a survey
No payment is made for completing Stats NZ surveys. The data is needed to produce vital and unbiased information for New Zealand. It's collected to benefit the country, just like taxes collected by Inland Revenue.

## Get information about a specific survey
List of Stats NZ surveys links you to specific information about all the surveys we run.
Get in touch if you:
- can't find the information you need
- need help completing a survey questionnaire
- have a question about a survey
- need to change the date and/or time of your appointment with an interviewer.
Contact us for survey help
